repository.name: agency-engineering-skills
repository.description: Normative promises for the skill packages and the interface in this repository, assessed against the packaging, documentation, and architectural conventions the repository's own skills state about themselves.

/interface.title: The interface renders only previously generated data
/interface.statement: Subjects within this domain OUGHT to render only data previously generated by a skill's own tooling, and OUGHT NOT recompute domain logic -- validation, inheritance, trust, or scoring -- in the browser.

/skills.title: Skills declare a manifest
/skills.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to declare a SKILL.md file at their root stating the skill's name, description, and operating instructions.

/skills.title: Skills document non-trivial workflows
/skills.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide reference documentation for any non-trivial workflow, rather than relying on SKILL.md alone.

/skills.title: Skills verify themselves automatically
/skills.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide an automated, repeatable way to check their own correctness -- a test suite or a bundled validator -- rather than depending on manual review alone.

/skills.title: Skills behave according to their declared dependency strength
/skills.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to behave according to the dependency strength declared for them in agency-engineering.yaml: a `required` dependency OUGHT to block the skill from running when that dependency is unavailable; a `recommended` dependency OUGHT to offer to run the dependency first and continue standalone if the user declines; an `optional` dependency OUGHT to be used silently when present, without prompting.

/skills/hypothesize.statement: Subjects within this domain OUGHT to keep capability status, evidence maturity, and scientific conclusion as three independent dimensions that are never conflated into one verdict.

/projects/downtake/research/methodology.title: Gold capture-depth labels come from labelers independent of case authorship
/projects/downtake/research/methodology.statement: The authoritative label used to score a case (the register or capture level a payload is judged to have reached) OUGHT to be assigned by a labeler or judge who did not author the case or the attack, and never by the model or party under test; an author's own guess OUGHT to be recorded separately from the authoritative label and OUGHT to carry no evidential weight on its own.

/projects/downtake/research/methodology.title: Coding rubrics are pre-registered before external cases are coded
/projects/downtake/research/methodology.statement: A rubric or codebook that governs how cases are labeled or how a study's metrics are defined OUGHT to be frozen, versioned, and dated before the corpus it governs is coded or labeled, so that codes are applied against a fixed rule rather than reverse-engineered to fit a desired result.

/projects/downtake/research/methodology.title: Single-coder or same-model-family findings are marked provisional pending independent replication
/projects/downtake/research/methodology.statement: A quantitative finding produced by a single coder, or by raters drawn from the same model family, OUGHT to be explicitly labeled provisional or an upper bound rather than a settled result, and OUGHT to state what independent replication would be required before the finding is treated as citable evidence.

/projects/downtake/research/reporting.title: Fabricated or illustrative example numbers are labeled non-evidentiary
/projects/downtake/research/reporting.statement: Numeric output produced from fabricated, synthetic, or illustrative run data OUGHT to be clearly labeled as non-evidentiary at the point of presentation, rather than allowed to read as a measured empirical result.

/projects/downtake/software/rcpi.title: RCPI ships with zero third-party runtime dependencies
/projects/downtake/software/rcpi.statement: The rcpi package OUGHT to declare zero third-party runtime dependencies, so it can be dropped into any evaluation harness without introducing a dependency conflict.

/projects/downtake/software/rcpi.title: RCPI never adjudicates its own labels
/projects/downtake/software/rcpi.statement: The rcpi scorer OUGHT to remain a pure function of externally supplied labeled cases and independently labeled run results, and OUGHT NOT to call a model or generate its own capture/disclosure labels internally as part of scoring.

/projects/downtake/software/rcpi.title: Reports built from synthetic or self-judged provenance are stamped invalid and suppress headline findings
/projects/downtake/software/rcpi.statement: A Report computed over RunResults whose provenance is not human_labeled or independent_judge_model OUGHT to be marked valid_as_evidence=False, OUGHT to carry an explicit warning naming the offending provenance, and its headline numbers OUGHT NOT to be presented as measured findings without that warning surfaced alongside them.

/projects/downtake/software/rcpi/adapter.title: RCPI ships with zero third-party runtime dependencies
/projects/downtake/software/rcpi/adapter.statement: The rcpi package OUGHT to declare zero third-party runtime dependencies, so it can be dropped into any evaluation harness without introducing a dependency conflict.

/projects/downtake/software/rcpi/adapter.title: RCPI never adjudicates its own labels
/projects/downtake/software/rcpi/adapter.statement: The rcpi scorer OUGHT to remain a pure function of externally supplied labeled cases and independently labeled run results, and OUGHT NOT to call a model or generate its own capture/disclosure labels internally as part of scoring.

/projects/downtake/software/rcpi/adapter.title: Reports built from synthetic or self-judged provenance are stamped invalid and suppress headline findings
/projects/downtake/software/rcpi/adapter.statement: A Report computed over RunResults whose provenance is not human_labeled or independent_judge_model OUGHT to be marked valid_as_evidence=False, OUGHT to carry an explicit warning naming the offending provenance, and its headline numbers OUGHT NOT to be presented as measured findings without that warning surfaced alongside them.

/projects/downtake/software/rcpi/adapter.title: Adapters refuse to fabricate a disclosure/silence judgment
/projects/downtake/software/rcpi/adapter.statement: An adapter that converts an external harness's output into RCPI runs OUGHT to require an explicit, provenance-tagged disclosure labeler in order to produce a valid silent-capture number, and OUGHT to fall back to a heuristic stamped synthetic_illustration (never a valid evidentiary provenance) when no such labeler is supplied.

/projects/graphist/analysis/ltp.title: The ltp causal model is publishable (zero error-severity diagnostics)
/projects/graphist/analysis/ltp.statement: ltp/ltp-model.yaml OUGHT to have zero error-severity diagnostics under `ltp validate` (structural errors and failed Categories-of-Legitimate-Reservation checks) before its generated projections under ltp/generated/ are treated as current; warning- and info-severity diagnostics are an acceptable standing scrutiny backlog, not a publication blocker.

/projects/graphist/research/experiment-g.title: A frozen confirmatory protocol cannot be changed after final-test unlock
/projects/graphist/research/experiment-g.statement: Once a confirmatory experiment protocol is frozen, it OUGHT to become an immutable, content-addressed artifact; the system OUGHT to reject any attempt to freeze a protocol after final-test computation, reject an incomplete protocol, and treat repeated freezes of the same protocol as idempotent.

/projects/graphist/research/publication.title: Capability status, evidence maturity, and scientific conclusion are never conflated
/projects/graphist/research/publication.statement: The generated research publication (research/generated/research-status.json and its README/use-cases/website projections) OUGHT to derive capability status (from tagged acceptance scenarios), evidence maturity (from evidence artifacts and evidence-role tags), and scientific conclusion (only from qualified, preregistered study outcomes) as three independent fields; a passing test OUGHT never to move a scientific conclusion, and an unqualified or non-preregistered outcome-bearing artifact OUGHT to be quarantined rather than silently promoted.

/projects/graphist/research/publication.title: The committed research publication is byte-reproducible from its sources
/projects/graphist/research/publication.statement: research/generated/research-status.json, the README.md and use-cases/README.md generated regions, and graphist_website/generated/research-data.js OUGHT to be exactly reproducible (byte-for-byte) by re-deriving them from research/portfolio.toml, the acceptance report, and the deterministic result collector; a committed copy that differs from a fresh re-derivation is stale.

/projects/graphist/software/formal/rocq.title: Rocq differential fixtures are a deterministic regeneration of the Python kernel
/projects/graphist/software/formal/rocq.statement: formal/theories/Differential/Fixtures.v OUGHT to be exactly reproducible by regenerating it from the Python kernel's verdicts (`graphist emit-differential`); a committed copy that differs from a fresh regeneration means the Rocq checker and the Python kernel have silently diverged.

/projects/graphist/software/python/archi.title: Gherkin step definitions call only the public application facade
/projects/graphist/software/python/archi.statement: Feature files and their step definitions OUGHT to describe actor goals and observable application outcomes only; step definitions OUGHT never to import or call a domain entity, repository, filesystem, hash implementation, or CLI command directly.

/projects/graphist/software/python/archi.title: DDD layers depend inward, never outward
/projects/graphist/software/python/archi.statement: Python modules under src/graphist OUGHT to import only from their own DDD layer or a layer closer to the domain (presentation -> application -> domain; infrastructure -> application -> domain), and the domain layer OUGHT to import nothing from files, command lines, model frameworks, or Behave.

/projects/graphist/software/python/lint.title: Python source is ruff-clean under the configured rule set
/projects/graphist/software/python/lint.statement: Python source in this repository OUGHT to pass `ruff check .` under the configured rule selection (E, F, I, UP, B, SIM) at line-length 100, target-version py311.

/projects/graphist/software/python/typin.title: The graphist package type-checks under mypy --strict
/projects/graphist/software/python/typin.statement: Python modules under src/graphist OUGHT to satisfy `mypy --strict` (as configured by [tool.mypy] in pyproject.toml) with no untyped defs, no incomplete defs, and no implicit Any generics. bgamma is included in every mypy run but is explicitly not held to that same strictness while its migration to full typing is incomplete; that narrower scope is a deliberate, documented exception, not a silent gap.

/projects/graphist/software/testing/unit.title: The pytest unit and application suite passes
/projects/graphist/software/testing/unit.statement: The pytest suite under tests/ OUGHT to pass with zero failures and zero errors on every supported Python version (3.11, 3.12, 3.13 per the CI matrix).

/projects/oracular-ui/oracular/diligence.title: Every competing explanation is scored under the same checklist
/projects/oracular-ui/oracular/diligence.statement: Every competing explanation (hypothesis) considered for a diligence question OUGHT to be scored against the same slot/rubric structure as every other explanation for that question, so that no single narrative receives privileged evidentiary treatment and disconfirming stories are not given a "free pass".

/projects/oracular-ui/oracular/diligence.title: Missing-explanation and missing-evidence uncertainty stay distinct
/projects/oracular-ui/oracular/diligence.statement: When a diligence session runs in open-world mode, the system OUGHT to keep "we might be missing a story" (a missing-explanation / H_NOA residual) and "we know the story types but lack data" (a missing-evidence / H_UND residual) as distinct, separately labeled hypotheses rather than collapsing them into one undifferentiated "unknown" bucket.

/projects/oracular-ui/oracular/diligence.title: Key claims carry a path back to underlying evidence
/projects/oracular-ui/oracular/diligence.statement: Every key claim (Slot) surfaced to a reviewer OUGHT to carry a machine-checkable reference (evidenceRefs) to the underlying evidence items it depends on, so a reviewer can trace the claim back to its source.

/projects/oracular-ui/oracular/governanc.title: Role-based access decisions are authorized by the backend, not the client alone
/projects/oracular-ui/oracular/governanc.statement: A decision to show, hide, or permit an action on sensitive evidence or governance controls OUGHT to be authorized by a backend access-control check for the current user and role, rather than derived solely from a client-side role flag the same client also set.

/projects/oracular-ui/oracular/governanc.title: Export is blocked when required claims or checks are not actually satisfied
/projects/oracular-ui/oracular/governanc.statement: Producing an IC-ready export OUGHT to be blocked whenever a required claim lacks a citation, a required slot is unscored, or another displayed governance check (scope completeness, replay integrity, injection guard) does not actually hold for the current deal -- the export control OUGHT to reflect the deal's real, computed state rather than a fixed display.

/projects/oracular-ui/oracular/governanc.title: Runs produce a real, deterministically verifiable audit record
/projects/oracular-ui/oracular/governanc.statement: Each diligence run OUGHT to produce a tamper-evident audit record whose evidence hash, config hash, and result hash are actually computed from the run's inputs and outputs, such that the same evidence and config reproducibly yield the same hashes and result ("replay determinism") -- not a label asserting this without a computation behind it.

/projects/oracular-ui/oracular/governanc.title: Enabling redaction actually masks sensitive content before it is sent
/projects/oracular-ui/oracular/governanc.statement: When redaction mode is enabled, sensitive tokens in evidence previews and submitted payloads OUGHT actually to be masked before leaving the client, and the evidence's redaction status OUGHT to reflect whether masking was actually applied to that item -- not a fixed label applied uniformly regardless of content.

/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.title: Reported research verdicts state an explicit, falsifiable claim boundary
/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.statement: A published PPL research verdict (H3 and its subclaims, and any future phase's headline hypothesis) OUGHT to be reported as a set of explicit subclaims each marked supported, not-supported, or open -- never as one undifferentiated accept/reject verdict -- so that a negative or partial result cannot be absorbed into an apparently positive headline claim.

/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.title: Human-readable reports disclose material warnings archived in machine evidence
/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.statement: Where `benchmarks/experiment_e.json` archives synthesis-tool warnings (e.g. Yosys "multiple conflicting drivers" warnings) against a backend's mapped-resource count, the corresponding human-readable report (EXPERIMENT_E.md) OUGHT to disclose those warnings, or an explicit pointer to them, wherever it presents that backend's resource counts as comparative evidence -- so a reader of the prose cannot mistake an unaudited count for an audited one.

/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.title: Reported research verdicts state an explicit, falsifiable claim boundary
/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.statement: A published PPL research verdict (H3 and its subclaims, and any future phase's headline hypothesis) OUGHT to be reported as a set of explicit subclaims each marked supported, not-supported, or open -- never as one undifferentiated accept/reject verdict -- so that a negative or partial result cannot be absorbed into an apparently positive headline claim.

/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.title: Human-readable reports disclose material warnings archived in machine evidence
/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.statement: Where `benchmarks/experiment_e.json` archives synthesis-tool warnings (e.g. Yosys "multiple conflicting drivers" warnings) against a backend's mapped-resource count, the corresponding human-readable report (EXPERIMENT_E.md) OUGHT to disclose those warnings, or an explicit pointer to them, wherever it presents that backend's resource counts as comparative evidence -- so a reader of the prose cannot mistake an unaudited count for an audited one.

/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.title: Goal-Tree leaves declare an observable satisfaction criterion
/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.statement: Every Goal-Tree leaf (necessary condition or critical success factor with no necessary condition of its own) in the project's LTP causal model OUGHT to declare an observable `satisfaction_criterion` -- a stated way to tell, from repository evidence, whether the condition currently holds -- rather than remaining an unfalsifiable assertion.

/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.title: Reported research verdicts state an explicit, falsifiable claim boundary
/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.statement: A published PPL research verdict (H3 and its subclaims, and any future phase's headline hypothesis) OUGHT to be reported as a set of explicit subclaims each marked supported, not-supported, or open -- never as one undifferentiated accept/reject verdict -- so that a negative or partial result cannot be absorbed into an apparently positive headline claim.

/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.title: Human-readable reports disclose material warnings archived in machine evidence
/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.statement: Where `benchmarks/experiment_e.json` archives synthesis-tool warnings (e.g. Yosys "multiple conflicting drivers" warnings) against a backend's mapped-resource count, the corresponding human-readable report (EXPERIMENT_E.md) OUGHT to disclose those warnings, or an explicit pointer to them, wherever it presents that backend's resource counts as comparative evidence -- so a reader of the prose cannot mistake an unaudited count for an audited one.

/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.title: Experiment F comparative thresholds are declared before final measurement
/projects/persistent-partition-logic/res.statement: Phase IV (Experiment F) OUGHT to declare its comparative pass/fail thresholds (e.g. throughput-per-area or energy-per-task ratios against the best baseline) before any final measurement is taken, so that a threshold cannot be tuned to whatever the measurement happens to show.

/projects/persistent-partition-logic/sof.title: RTL cells flag invalid commands combinationally and hold state
/projects/persistent-partition-logic/sof.statement: Every generated RTL cell OUGHT to assert `invalid_command` combinationally for a bad opcode (values 5-7), an empty merge mask, an empty or full cut mask, or an out-of-range `load_state`, and OUGHT to hold the current state on that cycle regardless of the offending command.

/projects/persistent-partition-logic/sof.title: RTL cells recover an invalid state encoding fail-closed to state 0
/projects/persistent-partition-logic/sof.statement: A generated RTL cell OUGHT to assert `invalid_state` whenever its register holds an unrepresentable encoding (compact code >= B_n, or one-hot popcount != 1), OUGHT to read `eq_matrix` as bottom while invalid, and OUGHT to recover to state ID 0 (the discrete partition) on the next clock edge regardless of the concurrent command.

/projects/persistent-partition-logic/sof.title: PRMC cell-interface commands reject all out-of-domain input, unchanged
/projects/persistent-partition-logic/sof.statement: `PRMC` cell-interface methods (Layer 2, Algorithm 1 section 5.1) OUGHT to reject every out-of-domain command with `ValueError` -- merge masks that are not nonempty subsets of the terminal set, cut masks as in the pure layer, and LOAD payloads that fail coverage, disjointness, or nonempty-block validation -- and OUGHT to leave the cell's state unchanged on rejection (the methods are pure; no new cell is produced).

/projects/persistent-partition-logic/sof.title: Pure cut() rejects empty, full, and unknown-terminal masks
/projects/persistent-partition-logic/sof.statement: The module-level pure function `cut(pi, S, T_n)` OUGHT to reject with `ValueError` an empty mask, a mask equal to the full terminal set (`S = T_n`), and a mask containing any terminal unknown to `T_n`, because Definition 3.5 requires a nonempty proper subset (`empty-set != S proper-subset T_n`).

/projects/persistent-partition-logic/sof.title: Pure merge() is total over arbitrary masks
/projects/persistent-partition-logic/sof.statement: The module-level pure function `merge(pi, S)` OUGHT to be total: when the mask `S` touches no block of the partition (an empty mask, or a mask containing only terminals unknown to the partition), `merge` OUGHT to return the partition unchanged rather than raising, and OUGHT never to insert unknown terminals into the returned state.

/projects/thrival/product/domain-languag.title: Path/Promise Loop modules use canonical Finder/Guide/Promise/Path language
/projects/thrival/product/domain-languag.statement: Code that implements the Path and Promise Loop model (a Guide authors a Path; a Finder owns a Path Instance made of Promise Loops with stages Imagine, Understand, Promise, Act, Reflect) OUGHT to name its identities, types, and directories using the canonical Finder/Guide/ Promise/Path vocabulary specified in `features/domain_language.feature`, not the legacy client/coach vocabulary that feature explicitly excludes ("legacy role names are not part of the canonical domain").

/projects/thrival/product/journeys.title: Journey documents validate against the canonical station/action schema
/projects/thrival/product/journeys.statement: A JourneyDocument (a Growth Map / Promise Loop instance authored for or by a Finder, made of `imagine | understand | promise | act | reflect` stations and habit/task actions carrying an `ActionPromise`) OUGHT to validate against the canonical schema in `src/lib/keepable/journeys/validation.ts` before the runtime or any persistence path treats it as an executable journey. This is a promise about the host-owned `JourneyDocument` type; it does not redefine or duplicate that type, and Promisify claims no authority over what a station, action, or `ActionPromise` means.

/projects/thrival/product/paths.title: Gherkin Path/Promise Loop specs exercise the production implementation
/projects/thrival/product/paths.statement: A Gherkin feature under `features/*.feature` that describes the Path or Promise Loop model (`@model` tag) OUGHT to have step definitions that exercise the production implementation in `src/lib/paths` and `src/lib/keepable`, so that a passing scenario is evidence about the shipped application rather than about an independent fixture.

/projects/thrival/software/supabase.title: Privileged Supabase edge functions require JWT verification
/projects/thrival/software/supabase.statement: A Supabase edge function that acts on behalf of a signed-in Finder/Guide identity, or that is invoked with a service-role bearer token from a database trigger, OUGHT to set `verify_jwt = true` for its entry in `supabase/config.toml`. A function OUGHT to be left `verify_jwt = false` only when its own handler is the thing establishing trust for a genuinely public/anonymous caller (for example a public booking-slot lookup, a public inquiry form, or a signed webhook whose provenance is verified by a separate signature check).

/projects/thrival/software/supabase.title: Tables holding personal or payment data enforce Row Level Security
/projects/thrival/software/supabase.statement: A Postgres table under `public` that stores a Finder's or Guide's personal data, session/journey content, or payment/financial records OUGHT to have Row Level Security enabled and OUGHT to carry policies that restrict reads and writes to the owning identity or an explicitly authorized role, rather than relying on the anon/service key boundary alone.

/projects/uptake-integrity-protocol/upta.title: Published research status is derived, never hand-edited or asserted
/projects/uptake-integrity-protocol/upta.statement: The published research status of this project (the generated hypothesis table in README.md and research/generated/research-status.json) OUGHT to be produced solely by deterministic derivation from research/portfolio.toml, research/evidence/, and the behave acceptance report, and OUGHT never be hand-edited, asserted in prose, or changed by a documentation edit alone.

/projects/uptake-integrity-protocol/upta.title: Published research status is derived, never hand-edited or asserted
/projects/uptake-integrity-protocol/upta.statement: The published research status of this project (the generated hypothesis table in README.md and research/generated/research-status.json) OUGHT to be produced solely by deterministic derivation from research/portfolio.toml, research/evidence/, and the behave acceptance report, and OUGHT never be hand-edited, asserted in prose, or changed by a documentation edit alone.

/projects/uptake-integrity-protocol/upta.title: Hidden control-status labels never reach evaluator-visible channels
/projects/uptake-integrity-protocol/upta.statement: Per-packet control status (defective / clean control / ambiguous) and other hidden gold-region labels OUGHT never appear in evaluator-visible packet JSON, prompts, API responses, CLI packet output, or the web UI; they OUGHT to live only in data/gold.

/projects/uptake-integrity-protocol/upta.title: Published research status is derived, never hand-edited or asserted
/projects/uptake-integrity-protocol/upta.statement: The published research status of this project (the generated hypothesis table in README.md and research/generated/research-status.json) OUGHT to be produced solely by deterministic derivation from research/portfolio.toml, research/evidence/, and the behave acceptance report, and OUGHT never be hand-edited, asserted in prose, or changed by a documentation edit alone.

/projects/uptake-integrity-protocol/upta.title: The audit log is append-only
/projects/uptake-integrity-protocol/upta.statement: docs/AUDIT_LOG.md OUGHT to be append-only: corrections OUGHT to be recorded as new, dated, sequentially numbered entries, and existing entries OUGHT never be silently edited or deleted.

/projects/uptake-integrity-protocol/upta.title: Published research status is derived, never hand-edited or asserted
/projects/uptake-integrity-protocol/upta.statement: The published research status of this project (the generated hypothesis table in README.md and research/generated/research-status.json) OUGHT to be produced solely by deterministic derivation from research/portfolio.toml, research/evidence/, and the behave acceptance report, and OUGHT never be hand-edited, asserted in prose, or changed by a documentation edit alone.

/projects/uptake-integrity-protocol/upta.title: Published surfaces use bounded, non-certification language
/projects/uptake-integrity-protocol/upta.statement: Published surfaces owned by this package (README.md and docs/*.md) OUGHT never assert that the package or a scored system is "UptakeBench-certified," "evidence-valid," "epistemically safe," "trustworthy," "honest," "generally reliable," "safe to deploy," or "proven trustworthy," and OUGHT not claim the package validates a model's own evaluations.
