promises.name: _renders_only_generated_data
promises.title: The interface renders only previously generated data
promises.statement: Subjects within this domain OUGHT to render only data previously generated by a skill's own tooling, and OUGHT NOT recompute domain logic -- validation, inheritance, trust, or scoring -- in the browser.

criteria.instructions: Confirm the interface reads a normalized JSON manifest per skill through its ResourceSource contract, and that no plugin view recomputes the skill's own derivations.

source.rationale: agency-ui/README.md states "The shell renders whatever the engines previously generated; it never re-derives anything," and read-only sources declare `determinism: static` with no `apply` capability.

promises.name: _declares_skill_manifest
promises.title: Skills declare a manifest
promises.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to declare a SKILL.md file at their root stating the skill's name, description, and operating instructions.

criteria.instructions: Confirm SKILL.md exists at the skill's root and its YAML front matter declares non-empty name and description fields.

source.rationale: Every skill in this repository is packaged and discovered through a SKILL.md front-matter contract; see ltp/SKILL.md, hypothesize/SKILL.md, and promisify/SKILL.md.

promises.name: _documents_references
promises.title: Skills document non-trivial workflows
promises.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide reference documentation for any non-trivial workflow, rather than relying on SKILL.md alone.

criteria.instructions: Confirm a references/ directory exists with at least one document per major workflow the SKILL.md delegates detail to.

source.rationale: Each existing skill's SKILL.md explicitly delegates workflow detail to a references/ directory rather than inlining it.

promises.name: _provides_automated_verification
promises.title: Skills verify themselves automatically
promises.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide an automated, repeatable way to check their own correctness -- a test suite or a bundled validator -- rather than depending on manual review alone.

criteria.instructions: Run the package's bundled test suite or validator and confirm it completes and reports a pass/fail result.

source.rationale: ltp and hypothesize each ship a pytest suite exercising their own engine; a skill without an equivalent automated check depends entirely on manual review to catch regressions in its own tooling.

promises.name: _respects_dependency_policy
promises.title: Skills behave according to their declared dependency strength
promises.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to behave according to the dependency strength declared for them in agency-engineering.yaml: a `required` dependency OUGHT to block the skill from running when that dependency is unavailable; a `recommended` dependency OUGHT to offer to run the dependency first and continue standalone if the user declines; an `optional` dependency OUGHT to be used silently when present, without prompting.

criteria.instructions: For each skill with a declared dependency in agency-engineering.yaml's spec.skills[].dependencies, confirm its SKILL.md states behavior matching the declared strength: `required` -> the skill will not proceed without the dependency; `recommended` -> the skill offers to run the dependency first, then continues standalone if declined; `optional` -> the skill uses the integration when present without prompting.

source.rationale: agency-engineering.yaml's spec.dependencyPolicy defines exactly these three strengths and their required behavior ("required: refuse to run when the dependency is unavailable", "recommended: offer to run the dependency first, then continue standalone if declined", "optional: use the integration when present without prompting"). Each skill that declares a dependency states matching behavior in its own SKILL.md's "Agency Engineering composition" section (e.g. ltp/SKILL.md and hypothesize/SKILL.md both state: "Promisify is recommended, not required... ask whether the user wants to run Promisify first. Continue standalone when the user declines.").

promises.name: _separates_capability_from_conclusion
promises.title: Capability, evidence, and conclusion stay separate
promises.statement: Subjects within this domain OUGHT to keep capability status, evidence maturity, and scientific conclusion as three independent dimensions that are never conflated into one verdict.

criteria.instructions: Confirm SKILL.md and the engine keep the three dimensions structurally distinct, and that a passing test alone cannot promote a scientific conclusion.

source.rationale: hypothesize/SKILL.md states "Capability != evidence != conclusion" and "Passing tests can never promote a hypothesis."

promises.name: _generated_artifacts_are_derived
promises.title: Generated LTP artifacts are derived, not authored
promises.statement: Subjects within this domain OUGHT to treat every file under a generated/ directory as derived output that tooling regenerates, and OUGHT NOT hand-edit it.

criteria.instructions: Confirm SKILL.md instructs against hand-editing generated/ output and that sync/check tooling exists to detect drift between the model and its generated projections.

source.rationale: ltp/SKILL.md states "Do not hand-edit anything under `generated/` -- it is machine-owned," and `ltp check` fails when a committed projection is stale or hand-edited.

promises.name: _promises_never_store_verdicts
promises.title: Promises never store a verdict
promises.statement: Subjects within this domain OUGHT to keep promise declarations free of any verdict, trust, or compliance state; only assessments may record kept/broken/trust.

criteria.instructions: Confirm the promise schema and validator reject a promise document containing status, verdict, kept, broken, trust, score, or compliance keys anywhere in its body.

source.rationale: promisify/SKILL.md's non-negotiable invariant #1 states a promise "never stores kept, broken, trust, compliance, or current truth," and `norms.py`'s FORBIDDEN_PROMISE_KEYS set enforces it at validation time.

promises.name: _gold_labels_are_independent_of_case_authorship
promises.title: Gold capture-depth labels come from labelers independent of case authorship
promises.statement: The authoritative label used to score a case (the register or capture level a payload is judged to have reached) OUGHT to be assigned by a labeler or judge who did not author the case or the attack, and never by the model or party under test; an author's own guess OUGHT to be recorded separately from the authoritative label and OUGHT to carry no evidential weight on its own.

criteria.instructions: Verify rcpi.schema.Case keeps `author_provisional_label` and `gold_label` as separate fields with `gold_label` starting empty/None, and that rcpi.metrics.score() warns when scoring proceeds without an independent gold label. Verify a labeling study's raters were blinded to case identity and did not author the specific cases they labeled (e.g. via a blinding/relabeling map).

source.reference: tool/rcpi/rcpi/schema.py lines 5-16 (module docstring) and the Case dataclass author_provisional_label/gold_label fields; tool/rcpi/README.md "Disciplines baked into the code" item 1 ("Don't grade your own homework"); studies/study2_interrater/README.md "Method" (blinding via make_sheet.py) and studies/study2_interrater/protocol.md
source.rationale: The tool's own README states this discipline explicitly, and the schema encodes it structurally as two separate dataclass fields rather than as a single mutable label; Study 2's blind_sheet.json / keymap.json implement the same discipline for the labeling pilot.

promises.name: _rubrics_are_preregistered_before_coding
promises.title: Coding rubrics are pre-registered before external cases are coded
promises.statement: A rubric or codebook that governs how cases are labeled or how a study's metrics are defined OUGHT to be frozen, versioned, and dated before the corpus it governs is coded or labeled, so that codes are applied against a fixed rule rather than reverse-engineered to fit a desired result.

criteria.instructions: Confirm the rubric/codebook document carries an explicit version, a frozen/pre-registration date, and a "fixed before coding" statement. Confirm a coding artifact it governs (e.g. mappings.json, labels.jsonl) declares itself as applying that rubric version and does not silently redefine the rule mid-study. Note whether independent evidence of temporal ordering exists (e.g. the rubric landing in an earlier commit than the coded data) or whether the ordering rests only on the document's own dated claim.

source.reference: tool/rcpi/rcpi/corpus/definitions.md (Version/Frozen/Status header); studies/study1_coverage/rubric.md (Version/Frozen/Status header); studies/study2_interrater/protocol.md (Version/Frozen/Purpose header)
source.rationale: All three documents state the same discipline in their own words ("pre-registration -- fixed BEFORE the external cases were coded" / "before labels are collected"), and the tool binds a published score to a hash of definitions.md via `rcpi hash` / `definitions_hash()` so a result is provably against one rubric version.

promises.name: _single_pass_findings_are_marked_provisional
promises.title: Single-coder or same-model-family findings are marked provisional pending independent replication
promises.statement: A quantitative finding produced by a single coder, or by raters drawn from the same model family, OUGHT to be explicitly labeled provisional or an upper bound rather than a settled result, and OUGHT to state what independent replication would be required before the finding is treated as citable evidence.

criteria.instructions: Check that any headline number in a study README is accompanied by an explicit statement of what would make it citable (e.g. an independent re-code, human raters instead of same-model-family raters) and is not presented as though it already settles the question it bears on.

source.reference: studies/study1_coverage/README.md "Limitations" and "What would make this citable (next actions)"; studies/study2_interrater/README.md "What this is -- read first" and "Why the high number is weak evidence (the honest read)"
source.rationale: Both study READMEs contain an explicit, structurally similar self-limiting section naming exactly this caveat in their own words (study1: "pending an independent re-code + per-register kappa before any number is citable"; study2: "an upper bound on agreement, not the human kappa the manual requires").

promises.name: _illustrative_numbers_are_labeled_not_evidence
promises.title: Fabricated or illustrative example numbers are labeled non-evidentiary
promises.statement: Numeric output produced from fabricated, synthetic, or illustrative run data OUGHT to be clearly labeled as non-evidentiary at the point of presentation, rather than allowed to read as a measured empirical result.

criteria.instructions: Verify that scoring/comparing tool/rcpi/rcpi/corpus/EXAMPLE_runs_illustrative.jsonl produces a Report with valid_as_evidence=False and a "NOT VALID AS EVIDENCE" warning, and that both README.md and tool/rcpi/README.md state in prose that these numbers are fabricated.
criteria.command: cd tool/rcpi && python -m rcpi compare rcpi/corpus/seed_cases.jsonl rcpi/corpus/EXAMPLE_runs_illustrative.jsonl

source.reference: README.md "Status" section ("The numbers shipped in EXAMPLE_runs_illustrative.jsonl are fabricated ... and are stamped invalid-as-evidence"); tool/rcpi/README.md Quickstart note
source.rationale: The root README makes this an explicit, direct claim about a specific named artifact rather than a general aspiration, so it is squarely assessable against that file's actual provenance field and the scorer's actual output.

promises.name: _is_zero_dependency
promises.title: RCPI ships with zero third-party runtime dependencies
promises.statement: The rcpi package OUGHT to declare zero third-party runtime dependencies, so it can be dropped into any evaluation harness without introducing a dependency conflict.

criteria.instructions: Confirm tool/rcpi/pyproject.toml's [project].dependencies list is empty, and that no module under tool/rcpi/rcpi/ imports a third-party package.

source.rationale: The package description and the README both assert zero-dependency status; pyproject.toml's dependencies field is the directly assessable ground truth for that claim.

promises.name: _never_self_adjudicates_labels
promises.title: RCPI never adjudicates its own labels
promises.statement: The rcpi scorer OUGHT to remain a pure function of externally supplied labeled cases and independently labeled run results, and OUGHT NOT to call a model or generate its own capture/disclosure labels internally as part of scoring.

criteria.instructions: Confirm rcpi.metrics.score() and the rcpi CLI take labels as input (RunResult.capture_level, .disclosed, .false_positive) and contain no model call, network call, or internal labeling heuristic. Note that rcpi.adapters.agentdojo.default_disclosure_heuristic is a separate, explicitly-marked-invalid convenience path outside the scorer itself -- see the sibling promise /software/rcpi/adapters/_refuses_to_fabricate_silent_capture, which governs that boundary.

source.reference: tool/rcpi/README.md "RCPI does not call any model ... It never adjudicates labels itself -- that would be grading its own homework"
source.rationale: Direct quote from the tool's own README describing its contract; metrics.py and cli.py contain only stdlib imports (dataclasses, collections, argparse, json, hashlib, pathlib), consistent with the claim.

promises.name: _suppresses_unqualified_evidence
promises.title: Reports built from synthetic or self-judged provenance are stamped invalid and suppress headline findings
promises.statement: A Report computed over RunResults whose provenance is not human_labeled or independent_judge_model OUGHT to be marked valid_as_evidence=False, OUGHT to carry an explicit warning naming the offending provenance, and its headline numbers OUGHT NOT to be presented as measured findings without that warning surfaced alongside them.

criteria.instructions: Confirm rcpi.metrics.score() sets valid_as_evidence from VALID_EVIDENCE_PROVENANCE = {HUMAN_LABELED, INDEPENDENT_JUDGE} and emits a "NOT VALID AS EVIDENCE" warning otherwise; confirm rcpi.scoring.render_report / render_comparison surface that flag prominently (a banner) rather than silently.

source.reference: tool/rcpi/rcpi/schema.py Provenance enum and VALID_EVIDENCE_PROVENANCE; tool/rcpi/rcpi/metrics.py score() provenance/validity block; tool/rcpi/rcpi/scoring.py render_report/render_comparison banners
source.rationale: This is, in the module's own words, "the manual's provenance-marking, applied to the tool itself: the tool will not let fabricated runs launder into a result" (schema.py module docstring).

promises.name: _refuses_to_fabricate_silent_capture
promises.title: Adapters refuse to fabricate a disclosure/silence judgment
promises.statement: An adapter that converts an external harness's output into RCPI runs OUGHT to require an explicit, provenance-tagged disclosure labeler in order to produce a valid silent-capture number, and OUGHT to fall back to a heuristic stamped synthetic_illustration (never a valid evidentiary provenance) when no such labeler is supplied.

criteria.instructions: Confirm convert() raises ValueError when exactly one of disclosure_fn/provenance is supplied, defaults to default_disclosure_heuristic + Provenance.SYNTHETIC_ILLUSTRATION with an explicit warning when neither is supplied, and that only a real disclosure_fn + a HUMAN_LABELED or INDEPENDENT_JUDGE provenance can reach a valid_as_evidence report.

source.reference: tool/rcpi/rcpi/adapters/agentdojo.py convert() docstring and body; tool/rcpi/tests/test_adapter_agentdojo.py test_heuristic_mode_is_not_evidence, test_independent_disclosure_labeler_gives_valid_silent_rate
source.rationale: README.md "In progress" section states this as a known, honestly disclosed limitation of the adapter: it "cannot produce the silent-capture endpoint without a supplied disclosure labeler ... so its heuristic mode is stamped invalid-as-evidence."

promises.name: _causal_model_has_no_error_severity_diagnostics
promises.title: The ltp causal model is publishable (zero error-severity diagnostics)
promises.statement: ltp/ltp-model.yaml OUGHT to have zero error-severity diagnostics under `ltp validate` (structural errors and failed Categories-of-Legitimate-Reservation checks) before its generated projections under ltp/generated/ are treated as current; warning- and info-severity diagnostics are an acceptable standing scrutiny backlog, not a publication blocker.

source.rationale: "A model is publishable when it has zero error-severity diagnostics." (ltp SKILL.md). At the time this promise was declared, ltp/ltp-model.yaml had just been migrated from a v1 (pre-typed) model and repaired to close 24 structural errors (missing necessity claims, a mis-migrated Evaporating Cloud, injections with no desirable-effect path, an invalid current_constraint, and others); see ltp/ltp-model.yaml.v1.bak for the pre-repair state.

promises.name: _confirmatory_protocol_is_immutable_once_frozen
promises.title: A frozen confirmatory protocol cannot be changed after final-test unlock
promises.statement: Once a confirmatory experiment protocol is frozen, it OUGHT to become an immutable, content-addressed artifact; the system OUGHT to reject any attempt to freeze a protocol after final-test computation, reject an incomplete protocol, and treat repeated freezes of the same protocol as idempotent.

required_scenarios: Reject a protocol missing a confirmatory system arm
Reject a protocol frozen after final-test computation
Repeating the same freeze is idempotent

criteria.instructions: Run `python -m behave features/protocol.feature` and confirm every listed scenario passes.

source.reference: README.md (Conflicts and tradeoffs table, "Reproducibility vs. experimentation speed")
source.rationale: "Confirmatory protocol and artifacts are content-addressed and immutable; exploratory changes must receive new identities." (README.md).

promises.name: _capability_evidence_and_conclusion_stay_independent
promises.title: Capability status, evidence maturity, and scientific conclusion are never conflated
promises.statement: The generated research publication (research/generated/research-status.json and its README/use-cases/website projections) OUGHT to derive capability status (from tagged acceptance scenarios), evidence maturity (from evidence artifacts and evidence-role tags), and scientific conclusion (only from qualified, preregistered study outcomes) as three independent fields; a passing test OUGHT never to move a scientific conclusion, and an unqualified or non-preregistered outcome-bearing artifact OUGHT to be quarantined rather than silently promoted.

criteria.instructions: This promise is enforced by the shared `hypothesize` engine's derivation rules (see the hypothesize skill's references/publication-model.md "Authority boundaries" table), not by a repository-specific script. Assess it by inspecting research/generated/research-status.json's quarantined_evidence[] (should list every outcome-bearing artifact that is not both qualified and preregistered) and by confirming no hypothesis in hypotheses[] has a conclusion other than not_tested unless it is backed by such a result.

source.rationale: README.md states "These pathways are separate hypotheses. Success or failure on one must not be reported as success or failure of all the others," and docs/machine-commitments.md frames the whole project around claims that "travel with an evidence package sufficient for an independent checker to evaluate it" -- this promise is the research-publication instance of that same discipline, implemented by the `hypothesize` package that graphist consumes (see src/graphist/application/research_status.py and src/graphist/presentation/research.py).

promises.name: _generated_research_publication_is_reproducible
promises.title: The committed research publication is byte-reproducible from its sources
promises.statement: research/generated/research-status.json, the README.md and use-cases/README.md generated regions, and graphist_website/generated/research-data.js OUGHT to be exactly reproducible (byte-for-byte) by re-deriving them from research/portfolio.toml, the acceptance report, and the deterministic result collector; a committed copy that differs from a fresh re-derivation is stale.

criteria.command: graphist research-status --behave-report artifacts/research/behave.json --check (equivalently: hypothesize check, once [tool.hypothesize] is configured)
criteria.success: Exit code 0 ("generated research publication is current").

source.rationale: CI runs "graphist research-status --behave-report artifacts/research/behave.json --check" and fails the build on drift; `make research-check` runs the same check locally (Makefile).

promises.name: _differential_fixtures_match_the_kernel
promises.title: Rocq differential fixtures are a deterministic regeneration of the Python kernel
promises.statement: formal/theories/Differential/Fixtures.v OUGHT to be exactly reproducible by regenerating it from the Python kernel's verdicts (`graphist emit-differential`); a committed copy that differs from a fresh regeneration means the Rocq checker and the Python kernel have silently diverged.

criteria.command: python -c "from graphist.presentation.differential import emit_fixtures; import pathlib; pathlib.Path('formal/theories/Differential/Fixtures.v').write_text(emit_fixtures())" && git diff --exit-code formal/theories/Differential/Fixtures.v
criteria.success: git diff reports no changes after regeneration.

source.rationale: "A Python<->Rocq differential -- `graphist emit-differential` regenerates formal/theories/Differential/Fixtures.v from the Python kernel's verdicts; compiling it forces the Rocq checker to agree vector-by-vector." (README.md); enforced by CI's "Differential fixtures are up to date (deterministic regeneration)" step.

promises.name: _acceptance_tests_use_only_the_application_facade
promises.title: Gherkin step definitions call only the public application facade
promises.statement: Feature files and their step definitions OUGHT to describe actor goals and observable application outcomes only; step definitions OUGHT never to import or call a domain entity, repository, filesystem, hash implementation, or CLI command directly.

criteria.instructions: Review features/steps/*.py for imports or calls that reach past graphist.application/bgamma.application into a domain, infrastructure, or presentation (CLI) symbol. features/environment.py's tag-directed composition (selecting the isolated graphist or bgamma application per feature tag) is the mechanism that keeps this boundary enforceable.

source.rationale: "Feature files describe actor goals and observable application outcomes. They never invoke a domain entity, repository, filesystem, hash implementation, or CLI command. Step definitions call only the appropriate public application facade." (README.md, Application-only Gherkin section).

promises.name: _dependencies_point_toward_the_domain
promises.title: DDD layers depend inward, never outward
promises.statement: Python modules under src/graphist OUGHT to import only from their own DDD layer or a layer closer to the domain (presentation -> application -> domain; infrastructure -> application -> domain), and the domain layer OUGHT to import nothing from files, command lines, model frameworks, or Behave.

criteria.instructions: Parse every module under src/graphist with ast and collect its graphist-internal imports. A module in layer L is kept when every collected import resolves to a module in one of L's allowed layers. tests/test_architecture.py encodes this same rule and is the executable form of this promise's criteria.

source.rationale: "Dependencies always point toward the domain. The domain has no knowledge of files, command lines, model frameworks, or Behave." (README.md, Architecture section).

promises.name: _python_source_passes_ruff
promises.title: Python source is ruff-clean under the configured rule set
promises.statement: Python source in this repository OUGHT to pass `ruff check .` under the configured rule selection (E, F, I, UP, B, SIM) at line-length 100, target-version py311.

source.rationale: "[tool.ruff] line-length = 100; target-version = \"py311\"" and "[tool.ruff.lint] select = [\"E\", \"F\", \"I\", \"UP\", \"B\", \"SIM\"]" (pyproject.toml, lines 80-85), enforced in CI by the "Lint" step.

promises.name: _graphist_package_is_mypy_strict_clean
promises.title: The graphist package type-checks under mypy --strict
promises.statement: Python modules under src/graphist OUGHT to satisfy `mypy --strict` (as configured by [tool.mypy] in pyproject.toml) with no untyped defs, no incomplete defs, and no implicit Any generics. bgamma is included in every mypy run but is explicitly not held to that same strictness while its migration to full typing is incomplete; that narrower scope is a deliberate, documented exception, not a silent gap.

criteria.success: Exit code 0 with no reported errors for the graphist package.

source.rationale: "[tool.mypy] strict = true; packages = [\"graphist\", \"bgamma\"]" with an override comment stating bgamma's "public call surfaces remain gradually typed. The high-assurance graphist package remains fully strict." (pyproject.toml, lines 49-73).

promises.name: _unit_tests_pass
promises.title: The pytest unit and application suite passes
promises.statement: The pytest suite under tests/ OUGHT to pass with zero failures and zero errors on every supported Python version (3.11, 3.12, 3.13 per the CI matrix).

criteria.success: Exit code 0, zero failed and zero error test outcomes.

source.rationale: CI runs "python -m pytest -q" as a required, non-conditional step in the python job for every matrix entry (.github/workflows/ci.yml).

promises.name: _evaluates_explanations_symmetrically
promises.title: Every competing explanation is scored under the same checklist
promises.statement: Every competing explanation (hypothesis) considered for a diligence question OUGHT to be scored against the same slot/rubric structure as every other explanation for that question, so that no single narrative receives privileged evidentiary treatment and disconfirming stories are not given a "free pass".

criteria.instructions: Inspect src/lib/domain.ts for a single shared Hypothesis/Slot shape (no hypothesis-specific bonus fields) and inspect src/lib/repositories/localRepository.ts to confirm every hypothesis built for a deal (including alternates and residual H_NOA/H_UND hypotheses) is constructed through the same builder with the same rubric fields (methodology, data, alignment, assumptions). Evidence of a narrower rubric, hidden bonus field, or hypothesis-specific scoring path for exactly one named hypothesis would count against this promise.

source.rationale: "Symmetry prevents bias / Every hypothesis faces the same obligations; no focal privilege" (brand principle #2); docs/white-paper.org Section 8.4 "Impartiality symmetry: Every explanation must instantiate the same checklist template (slot parity)"; docs/one-pager.txt "Test each explanation with the same checklist so none gets special treatment."

promises.name: _separates_typed_residual_uncertainty
promises.title: Missing-explanation and missing-evidence uncertainty stay distinct
promises.statement: When a diligence session runs in open-world mode, the system OUGHT to keep "we might be missing a story" (a missing-explanation / H_NOA residual) and "we know the story types but lack data" (a missing-evidence / H_UND residual) as distinct, separately labeled hypotheses rather than collapsing them into one undifferentiated "unknown" bucket.

criteria.instructions: Inspect createDealSeeded in src/lib/repositories/localRepository.ts for two separate hypothesis records (H_NOA: "None of the above", H_UND: "Underdetermined") added only when input.worldMode === "open", and confirm abductioTypes.ts models gamma_noa/gamma_und as independent config parameters rather than a single merged residual parameter.

source.rationale: "Oracular never collapses 'we might be missing a story' and 'we need more data' into one bucket; it logs and displays them separately because they imply different next actions" (docs/improvements.org, section 3, mapping NOA/UND); docs/one-pager.txt "What we don't know yet" split into missing explanations vs missing evidence.

promises.name: _traces_claims_to_citations
promises.title: Key claims carry a path back to underlying evidence
promises.statement: Every key claim (Slot) surfaced to a reviewer OUGHT to carry a machine-checkable reference (evidenceRefs) to the underlying evidence items it depends on, so a reviewer can trace the claim back to its source.

criteria.instructions: Confirm the Slot type carries an evidenceRefs: string[] field, that Evidence records exist with stable ids, and that the UI (governance / evidence tabs in src/pages/Index.tsx) renders evidenceRefs as a resolvable link back to Evidence rather than free text. Note, but do not treat as disqualifying for this narrower "traceability exists" promise, that some seed Slots carry an empty evidenceRefs array; that gap is covered by the separate export-gating promise (/oracular/governance/_gates_export_on_citation_completeness).

source.rationale: "every claim traces to evidence, quote spans, and deterministic scoring logs" (Auditability principle); docs/one-pager.txt "transparent sourcing: every key claim points back to underlying evidence."

promises.name: _enforces_access_control_via_backend_authorization
promises.title: Role-based access decisions are authorized by the backend, not the client alone
promises.statement: A decision to show, hide, or permit an action on sensitive evidence or governance controls OUGHT to be authorized by a backend access-control check for the current user and role, rather than derived solely from a client-side role flag the same client also set.

criteria.instructions: Inspect hasGovernanceAccess and userRole in src/pages/Index.tsx and the sign-in/sign-up flow. Look for a network call to a backend authorization endpoint (e.g. matching the "the backend authorizes ..." / "the backend denies ... with reason rbac_denial" scenarios in features/ui_rbac_access_controls.feature) that determines what the current user may view or do. A role sourced only from window.localStorage, a self-service signup form, or a URL query parameter compared against a single hardcoded default token, with no backend call in the authorization path, is evidence the promise is broken.

source.rationale: The feature's own scenarios presuppose backend-enforced authorization: "Given the backend authorizes 'GET /runs/RUN123/evidence-bundle'" / "Given the backend denies ... with reason 'rbac_denial'" -- i.e. the acceptance suite's own normative model of RBAC requires a backend authorization decision, not a client-only role check. Also grounded in commit 7d72996 "add auth", which introduces the client-side role/session model being assessed.

promises.name: _gates_export_on_citation_completeness
promises.title: Export is blocked when required claims or checks are not actually satisfied
promises.statement: Producing an IC-ready export OUGHT to be blocked whenever a required claim lacks a citation, a required slot is unscored, or another displayed governance check (scope completeness, replay integrity, injection guard) does not actually hold for the current deal -- the export control OUGHT to reflect the deal's real, computed state rather than a fixed display.

criteria.instructions: Inspect the Export Gate modal in src/pages/Index.tsx (state showExportGate). Each checklist row ("All required slots scored", "Citations present", "No UNSCOPED roots", "Replayable run", "Injection guard") should be a boolean derived from the current deal's actual data (e.g. an unscored required slot, or a claim with an empty evidenceRefs array, should turn that row red and disable "Proceed to export"). A checklist that always renders every row as a static checkmark, or a "Proceed to export" button whose disabled state does not depend on that data, is evidence the promise is broken.

source.rationale: "'Policy-backed' behaviors (cannot export if claims lack citations)" (Ruler archetype, docs/brand.txt); features/10_export_gate.feature.

promises.name: _produces_deterministic_replayable_record
promises.title: Runs produce a real, deterministically verifiable audit record
promises.statement: Each diligence run OUGHT to produce a tamper-evident audit record whose evidence hash, config hash, and result hash are actually computed from the run's inputs and outputs, such that the same evidence and config reproducibly yield the same hashes and result ("replay determinism") -- not a label asserting this without a computation behind it.

criteria.instructions: Search the frontend for a hashing or digest computation (e.g. a crypto.subtle/sha256 call, or a call into a backend endpoint that returns a computed hash) feeding the "evidence manifest hash", "config hash", "result hash", and "Replayable" badges. Evidence hash strings that are hardcoded literals not derived from any run input would count against this promise.

source.rationale: "Determinism builds trust: Same evidence + config -> same outputs. Replay is a product feature." (brand.txt principle #4); docs/one-pager.txt "A replayable audit file: a tamper-evident record of inputs, evidence references, and rubric-based scoring"; docs/white-paper.org "deterministic scoring logs, tamper-evident audit artifacts... replay determinism"; features/07_replay_determinism.feature.

promises.name: _redacts_sensitive_content_before_egress
promises.title: Enabling redaction actually masks sensitive content before it is sent
promises.statement: When redaction mode is enabled, sensitive tokens in evidence previews and submitted payloads OUGHT actually to be masked before leaving the client, and the evidence's redaction status OUGHT to reflect whether masking was actually applied to that item -- not a fixed label applied uniformly regardless of content.

criteria.instructions: Search for a masking/redaction transform function applied to evidence text before render or before a network request, matching features/03_redaction_boundary.feature ("prompt previews mask sensitive tokens"). Evidence that an item's `redaction` field is assigned by a fixed rule unrelated to content inspection (e.g. "Applied" for every evidence item whose type is not "Link", with no scan of keyClaimContent for sensitive tokens) is evidence the promise is broken.

source.rationale: "Redaction options for sensitive content" (docs/one-pager.txt, Security & deployment); features/03_redaction_boundary.feature: "When redaction mode is enabled ... prompt previews mask sensitive tokens."

promises.name: _claim_boundary_is_explicit_and_falsifiable
promises.title: Reported research verdicts state an explicit, falsifiable claim boundary
promises.statement: A published PPL research verdict (H3 and its subclaims, and any future phase's headline hypothesis) OUGHT to be reported as a set of explicit subclaims each marked supported, not-supported, or open -- never as one undifferentiated accept/reject verdict -- so that a negative or partial result cannot be absorbed into an apparently positive headline claim.

criteria.instructions: Inspect SPEC.md section 9's claim-boundary table and EXPERIMENT_E.md's "Verdict (hypothesis H3)" table. Kept requires each subclaim (H3a-H3e) to carry its own status distinct from the others, with at least one subclaim explicitly marked not supported when the underlying evidence does not support it.

source.reference: SPEC.md section 9 (Normative claim boundary table); EXPERIMENT_E.md 'Verdict (hypothesis H3)'
source.rationale: The project's own roadmap (README.md Phase status) treats a negative Phase III result as a legitimate, preserved outcome rather than a failure to be hidden, which is the behavior this promise makes explicit and checkable.

promises.name: _reports_disclose_material_synthesis_warnings
promises.title: Human-readable reports disclose material warnings archived in machine evidence
promises.statement: Where `benchmarks/experiment_e.json` archives synthesis-tool warnings (e.g. Yosys "multiple conflicting drivers" warnings) against a backend's mapped-resource count, the corresponding human-readable report (EXPERIMENT_E.md) OUGHT to disclose those warnings, or an explicit pointer to them, wherever it presents that backend's resource counts as comparative evidence -- so a reader of the prose cannot mistake an unaudited count for an audited one.

criteria.instructions: Grep `benchmarks/experiment_e.json` for a nonempty `warnings` array on a synthesis record (e.g. `label_array` at n=3..5), then check whether EXPERIMENT_E.md's "Same-target iCE40 synthesis" section or its H3c verdict row mentions the warnings or the audit status of the affected backend's counts.

source.authority: agent-derived (PPL project's own prior LTP analysis)
source.reference: ltp/ltp-model.yaml entities UDE-5, ASM-6, ACT-2 (pre-existing project self-assessment)
source.rationale: The project's own prior LTP analysis already names this exact gap (UDE-5: 'the human Phase III report omits label-array synthesis warnings retained in JSON') and records a planned but unexecuted remediation (ACT-2). This promise makes that self-identified norm independently assessable rather than leaving it as an inferred causal node in one model.

promises.name: _necessary_conditions_have_satisfaction_criteria
promises.title: Goal-Tree leaves declare an observable satisfaction criterion
promises.statement: Every Goal-Tree leaf (necessary condition or critical success factor with no necessary condition of its own) in the project's LTP causal model OUGHT to declare an observable `satisfaction_criterion` -- a stated way to tell, from repository evidence, whether the condition currently holds -- rather than remaining an unfalsifiable assertion.

criteria.instructions: Assess via `ltp --root . validate`. Kept requires zero `GT-006` diagnostics ("goal-tree leaf has no observable satisfaction criterion") in the current model. This promise is declared before the project's v1 LTP model has been migrated to the v2 schema the installed `ltp` engine requires, so its first assessment is necessarily `unknown` until that migration and repair pass completes.

source.rationale: Demonstrates the sanctioned host-type-projection pattern: Promisify projects the LTP `necessary_condition`/Goal-Tree-leaf type into an inherited domain and assesses the project's own model against it, without duplicating LTP's entities or claiming its logical authority.

promises.name: _comparative_thresholds_declared_before_measurement
promises.title: Experiment F comparative thresholds are declared before final measurement
promises.statement: Phase IV (Experiment F) OUGHT to declare its comparative pass/fail thresholds (e.g. throughput-per-area or energy-per-task ratios against the best baseline) before any final measurement is taken, so that a threshold cannot be tuned to whatever the measurement happens to show.

criteria.instructions: Kept requires PHASE_IV.md (or a successor document) to state a numeric or otherwise falsifiable comparative threshold prior to the existence of any Experiment F measurement artifact under `benchmarks/` for Phase IV. Re-check whenever an Experiment F result is first produced, since the promise is only meaningfully testable once a measurement exists to check the declaration against.

source.reference: PHASE_IV.md 'Hypotheses' table (F5) and the paragraph beginning 'The thresholds for F5 must be declared before final measurements.'

promises.name: _invalid_command_holds_state
promises.title: RTL cells flag invalid commands combinationally and hold state
promises.statement: Every generated RTL cell OUGHT to assert `invalid_command` combinationally for a bad opcode (values 5-7), an empty merge mask, an empty or full cut mask, or an out-of-range `load_state`, and OUGHT to hold the current state on that cycle regardless of the offending command.

criteria.instructions: Evidence is the Experiment B RTL conformance sweep (`experiment_b.py`, `python3 -m pytest -q test_experiment_b.py`), which injects invalid opcode/mask classes per configuration and expects zero mismatches against the reference model across all injected invalid-code counts reported in EXPERIMENT_B.md's "invalid codes (c/o)" column.

source.reference: SPEC.md section 8 (Normative RTL interface, Phase II); EXPERIMENT_B.md claim C1/C5

promises.name: _invalid_state_recovers_fail_closed
promises.title: RTL cells recover an invalid state encoding fail-closed to state 0
promises.statement: A generated RTL cell OUGHT to assert `invalid_state` whenever its register holds an unrepresentable encoding (compact code >= B_n, or one-hot popcount != 1), OUGHT to read `eq_matrix` as bottom while invalid, and OUGHT to recover to state ID 0 (the discrete partition) on the next clock edge regardless of the concurrent command.

criteria.instructions: Evidence is Experiment B's claim C5 (reset and recovery): every unused compact code and sampled non-one-hot patterns (zero, double-hot, all-ones) are injected via the simulation backdoor (`PPL_SIM_BACKDOOR`), must be flagged the same cycle, and must recover to bottom on the next edge. Re-run with `python3 -m pytest -q test_experiment_b.py` (requires Icarus Verilog).

promises.name: _cell_interface_rejects_out_of_domain_commands
promises.title: PRMC cell-interface commands reject all out-of-domain input, unchanged
promises.statement: `PRMC` cell-interface methods (Layer 2, Algorithm 1 section 5.1) OUGHT to reject every out-of-domain command with `ValueError` -- merge masks that are not nonempty subsets of the terminal set, cut masks as in the pure layer, and LOAD payloads that fail coverage, disjointness, or nonempty-block validation -- and OUGHT to leave the cell's state unchanged on rejection (the methods are pure; no new cell is produced).

criteria.instructions: Run `python3 -m pytest -q test_experiment_a.py::test_cell_interface_rejects_invalid_commands`. Kept requires assertion that the cell's state is unchanged after each rejected command (not merely that an exception was raised).

promises.name: _pure_cut_rejects_invalid_masks
promises.title: Pure cut() rejects empty, full, and unknown-terminal masks
promises.statement: The module-level pure function `cut(pi, S, T_n)` OUGHT to reject with `ValueError` an empty mask, a mask equal to the full terminal set (`S = T_n`), and a mask containing any terminal unknown to `T_n`, because Definition 3.5 requires a nonempty proper subset (`empty-set != S proper-subset T_n`).

criteria.instructions: Run `python3 -m pytest -q test_experiment_a.py::test_pure_cut_rejects_bad_masks`. Kept requires the case to cover all three rejected classes (empty, full, and unknown-terminal masks) with `pytest.raises(ValueError)`.

source.reference: SPEC.md section 4 (Two-layer validation policy), section 6 erratum E2

promises.name: _pure_merge_is_total
promises.title: Pure merge() is total over arbitrary masks
promises.statement: The module-level pure function `merge(pi, S)` OUGHT to be total: when the mask `S` touches no block of the partition (an empty mask, or a mask containing only terminals unknown to the partition), `merge` OUGHT to return the partition unchanged rather than raising, and OUGHT never to insert unknown terminals into the returned state.

criteria.instructions: Run `python3 -m pytest -q test_experiment_a.py::test_pure_merge_is_total_per_appendix_a`. The promise is assessable as kept only if the case exercises both an empty mask and a mask of wholly-unknown terminals and asserts the partition is returned unchanged with no exception raised.

source.reference: SPEC.md section 4 (Two-layer validation policy), erratum E1
source.rationale: SPEC.md section 4 resolves erratum E1 (merge leniency) by fixing the pure layer to follow Appendix A's total semantics, in contrast to the stricter PRMC cell-interface layer.

promises.name: _uses_canonical_finder_guide_promise_path_language
promises.title: Path/Promise Loop modules use canonical Finder/Guide/Promise/Path language
promises.statement: Code that implements the Path and Promise Loop model (a Guide authors a Path; a Finder owns a Path Instance made of Promise Loops with stages Imagine, Understand, Promise, Act, Reflect) OUGHT to name its identities, types, and directories using the canonical Finder/Guide/ Promise/Path vocabulary specified in `features/domain_language.feature`, not the legacy client/coach vocabulary that feature explicitly excludes ("legacy role names are not part of the canonical domain").

criteria.instructions: Within the scoped paths, grep case-insensitively for `\bclient\b` and `\bcoach\b` used as a role/identity (type literals, enum values, directory names, function/hook names such as `useCoaches`, `CoachDirectoryProfile`) versus `\bfinder\b` and `\bguide\b`. A literal role-typed value such as `journey_author_type: "client" | "coach"` in the core `JourneyDocument`/`Journey` type is a clear violation; an import alias such as `@/integrations/supabase/client` (the Supabase API client, not a person) is not and should be excluded by hand from the count.

source.rationale: `features/domain_language.feature` is an authored `@model` scenario stating the canonical domain model as the target state for this exact module scope; it is the repository's own normative statement, not an externally imported convention.

promises.name: _journey_documents_validate_against_canonical_schema
promises.title: Journey documents validate against the canonical station/action schema
promises.statement: A JourneyDocument (a Growth Map / Promise Loop instance authored for or by a Finder, made of `imagine | understand | promise | act | reflect` stations and habit/task actions carrying an `ActionPromise`) OUGHT to validate against the canonical schema in `src/lib/keepable/journeys/validation.ts` before the runtime or any persistence path treats it as an executable journey. This is a promise about the host-owned `JourneyDocument` type; it does not redefine or duplicate that type, and Promisify claims no authority over what a station, action, or `ActionPromise` means.

criteria.instructions: Run `npx vitest run src/test/journeyDocument.test.ts` and confirm `parseJourneyDocument` / `isJourneyDocument` reject malformed inputs (missing `promise.required`/`promise.promised`, an unsupported `station_type`, an unsupported `task.component.component_type`) while accepting well-formed documents, including documents using the legacy `commitment`/`committed`/`commit` keys that `normalizeJourneyDocumentInput` is expected to migrate in place.

source.rationale: The repository already implements this exact contract with a Zod schema and an explicit legacy-key migration path (see `normalizeActionInput`, `OLD_PROMISE_KEY` handling); this promise names the expectation that the schema stays the enforcement point rather than being bypassed by ad hoc parsing elsewhere.

promises.name: _gherkin_specs_exercise_production_path_implementation
promises.title: Gherkin Path/Promise Loop specs exercise the production implementation
promises.statement: A Gherkin feature under `features/*.feature` that describes the Path or Promise Loop model (`@model` tag) OUGHT to have step definitions that exercise the production implementation in `src/lib/paths` and `src/lib/keepable`, so that a passing scenario is evidence about the shipped application rather than about an independent fixture.

criteria.instructions: Inspect `features/step_definitions/*.js` and `features/support/*.js` for imports of `src/lib/paths`, `src/lib/keepable`, or `src/features/keepable` (relative or `@/` aliased). A step-definition file that only builds and asserts against locally-defined plain-object helpers (no import from those directories) cannot license production implementation evidence for the Path/Promise Loop capabilities it appears to specify, even when its scenarios pass.

source.rationale: The features under features/*.feature (dynamic_path_loading, showing_up_path_authoring, showing_up_adaptive_routing, shared_onboarding_loop, promise_visibility_and_consent, domain_language) all carry `@model` and describe the same canonical Path/Promise Loop model that src/lib/paths and src/lib/keepable implement; a stated purpose of a BDD acceptance suite is to prove the shipped system behaves as specified, which requires the step definitions to call that system.

promises.name: _privileged_edge_functions_require_jwt_verification
promises.title: Privileged Supabase edge functions require JWT verification
promises.statement: A Supabase edge function that acts on behalf of a signed-in Finder/Guide identity, or that is invoked with a service-role bearer token from a database trigger, OUGHT to set `verify_jwt = true` for its entry in `supabase/config.toml`. A function OUGHT to be left `verify_jwt = false` only when its own handler is the thing establishing trust for a genuinely public/anonymous caller (for example a public booking-slot lookup, a public inquiry form, or a signed webhook whose provenance is verified by a separate signature check).

criteria.instructions: Run `npx vitest run src/test/launchSecurityFollowups.test.ts`, which pins the expected `verify_jwt` value for each privileged function against `supabase/config.toml`. Cross-check any function this test does not cover by tracing its callers: a `supabase.functions.invoke(...)` call from an authenticated page (e.g. src/pages/GuideDashboard.tsx, src/pages/ClientOnboarding.tsx) or a `net.http_post` call from a `SECURITY DEFINER` trigger that sends `Authorization: Bearer <service_role_key>` (e.g. supabase/migrations/20260309050023_*.sql) both warrant `verify_jwt = true`; a handler whose own code is the public/anonymous entry point (e.g. public-bookable-slots, submit-public-inquiry, track-public-event, stripe-webhook) does not.

source.rationale: This is not an imported best practice: the repository already encodes this exact expectation as a regression test (launch security follow-ups) and already applies `verify_jwt = true` to functions with an identical calling pattern (create-session-engagement, delete-account-data, export-account-data, google-calendar-*, process-email-queue).

promises.name: _tables_handling_personal_or_payment_data_enforce_row_level_security
promises.title: Tables holding personal or payment data enforce Row Level Security
promises.statement: A Postgres table under `public` that stores a Finder's or Guide's personal data, session/journey content, or payment/financial records OUGHT to have Row Level Security enabled and OUGHT to carry policies that restrict reads and writes to the owning identity or an explicitly authorized role, rather than relying on the anon/service key boundary alone.

criteria.instructions: Run the RLS-focused vitest suites: `npx vitest run src/test/securityRlsGuardrails.test.ts src/test/paymentRlsSecurityReviewFixes.test.ts src/test/offerCodeSecurityHardening.test.ts`. Cross-check by grepping `supabase/migrations` for `ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` and `CREATE POLICY` statements scoped to the table in question; a table introduced without a matching policy, or a migration that widens a policy to `USING (true)` for a personal-data table, is evidence against this promise.

source.rationale: Grounded in the repository's own recurring migration pattern: dozens of migrations are explicitly named for RLS hardening (e.g. security_gate_rls_hardening, payment_and_connect_rls_hardening, harden_payment_link_public_read, offer_code_hardening, followup_hardening_and_query_indexes), and dedicated vitest suites exist solely to guard this property.

promises.name: _research_status_is_derived_not_hand_edited
promises.title: Published research status is derived, never hand-edited or asserted
promises.statement: The published research status of this project (the generated hypothesis table in README.md and research/generated/research-status.json) OUGHT to be produced solely by deterministic derivation from research/portfolio.toml, research/evidence/, and the behave acceptance report, and OUGHT never be hand-edited, asserted in prose, or changed by a documentation edit alone.

criteria.success: `hypothesize check` (invoked via `make research-check`) exits zero, meaning re-derivation from the registry, evidence, and acceptance report reproduces the committed README and JSON publication byte-for-byte.
criteria.instructions: Run `make research-check` (which runs the behave acceptance suite then `hypothesize check`). A nonzero exit or a diff between the re-derived and committed publication is evidence the promise is broken.

source.rationale: "Any presentation layer ... must never decide research status itself" and "README or prose edit -> changes no computed status" (README.md, docs/research-publication.md).

promises.name: _excludes_hidden_labels_from_evaluator_channels
promises.title: Hidden control-status labels never reach evaluator-visible channels
promises.statement: Per-packet control status (defective / clean control / ambiguous) and other hidden gold-region labels OUGHT never appear in evaluator-visible packet JSON, prompts, API responses, CLI packet output, or the web UI; they OUGHT to live only in data/gold.

criteria.command: ! grep -l '"status"' data/packets/public/*.json && test "$(grep -l '"status"' data/gold/development/*.json | wc -l | tr -d ' ')" = "8"
criteria.success: No public packet file contains a "status" key, and exactly the eight development gold files carry it (the Makefile `hygiene` target).
criteria.instructions: Run `make hygiene` or the command above. Also see src/uptakebench/application.py's register_packet, which rejects any packet leaking "status" or "gold_path" into the public channel, and features/benchmark/packet_registration.feature's "Reject a packet that leaks a hidden status label."

source.rationale: docs/CORPUS_CARD.md's "Presentation rule" and docs/AUDIT_LOG.md AL-004 ("evaluator-visible packets disclosed their hidden status labels" — repaired in 0.2.0-dev).

promises.name: _audit_log_is_append_only
promises.title: The audit log is append-only
promises.statement: docs/AUDIT_LOG.md OUGHT to be append-only: corrections OUGHT to be recorded as new, dated, sequentially numbered entries, and existing entries OUGHT never be silently edited or deleted.

criteria.instructions: Compare docs/AUDIT_LOG.md's entry sequence (AL-001..AL-N) against its git history: entries must only be appended, never rewritten in place. A correction to an earlier finding must appear as a new entry (e.g. AL-006's "editorial erratum" left the disputed source unchanged and recorded the correction as its own entry) rather than an edit to the original.

source.rationale: docs/AUDIT_LOG.md front matter (`status: append_only`) and its own text: "This file is append-only. Corrections are new entries; existing entries are never silently edited."

promises.name: _candidate_authors_firewalled_from_hidden_scoring
promises.title: Candidate-method authors are firewalled from hidden content and final scoring
promises.statement: A candidate-method author OUGHT never control both hidden-set content and final scoring for a method arm; this firewall OUGHT to be established and disclosed before any candidate-method arm runs.

criteria.instructions: Inspect docs/ROLES_AND_CONFLICTS.md's role table for "Candidate-method development" and its conflict disclosure ("Candidate-method authors may not control hidden content or final scoring"). Kept requires a disclosed, checked firewall before a method arm runs; not_applicable while no candidate-method arm exists yet.

source.rationale: docs/ROLES_AND_CONFLICTS.md's role table and "Minimum panel shape" section: "A candidate-method author may not control both hidden-set content and final scoring."

promises.name: _uses_bounded_noncertification_language
promises.title: Published surfaces use bounded, non-certification language
promises.statement: Published surfaces owned by this package (README.md and docs/*.md) OUGHT never assert that the package or a scored system is "UptakeBench-certified," "evidence-valid," "epistemically safe," "trustworthy," "honest," "generally reliable," "safe to deploy," or "proven trustworthy," and OUGHT not claim the package validates a model's own evaluations.

criteria.success: The command reports PASS for every file with no prohibited-pattern matches.
criteria.instructions: Run the reporting linter (src/uptakebench/reporting.py PROHIBITED_PATTERNS) against README.md and docs/*.md via `make hygiene` or the command above.

source.rationale: README.md's "Boundaries" section and CHANGELOG's "non-certification reporting linter" entry; enforced by REQ-NON-CERTIFICATION / CAP-NON-CERTIFICATION in research/portfolio.toml.

promises.name: _phase0_pilot_runs_only_on_frozen_independent_artifacts
promises.title: Phase-0 pilot runs only against frozen, hashed, independent artifacts
promises.statement: A Phase-0 pilot run OUGHT to execute only against frozen, content-hashed, independently produced packet, Gold Region, schema, scorer, instruction, and configuration artifacts, recorded in an immutable run manifest dated before any data inspection.

criteria.instructions: features/studies/phase0_activation.feature encodes this rule as "Reject activation before independent Gold Regions exist," "Reject activation dated after data inspection," and "Activate a completely frozen pilot," but every scenario is currently tagged @wip and skipped, so the enforcement mechanism (CAP-PILOT-ACTIVATION, CAP-RUN-MANIFEST) does not yet exist. Assess by running `make bdd` and checking whether these scenarios are skipped, passing, or failing, and by checking whether any pilot activation has actually occurred.

source.rationale: Mirrors research/portfolio.toml's REQ-PILOT-FROZEN-ARTIFACTS (capabilities CAP-PILOT-ACTIVATION, CAP-RUN-MANIFEST), specified but not built per docs/project/status.md.

promises.name: _requires_challenge_record_per_packet_before_alpha
promises.title: Alpha release requires one independent challenge record per packet
promises.statement: Before an Alpha release, every benchmark packet in data/packets/public OUGHT to have one independent challenge record naming a challenger independent of packet drafting and candidate-method development.

criteria.instructions: Every packet under data/packets/public OUGHT to have a corresponding challenge record under data/adjudication naming an independent challenger. Count packets and count independent challenge records; the promise is kept only when the counts match and each record's challenger is disclosed as independent per docs/ROLES_AND_CONFLICTS.md's confirmation-entry template.

assessments.rationale: hasGovernanceAccess (src/pages/Index.tsx line 799) is `["manager", "compliance", "admin"].includes(normalizedRole)`, where normalizedRole comes entirely from client-side state (userRole) set by loadAuthSession/saveAuthSession, which read and write window.localStorage. Sign-up (~lines 1005-1019) lets any visitor create an account and grants the "admin" role client-side whenever signupAsAdmin is checked and adminInviteGranted is true; adminInviteGranted becomes true when a URL query parameter admin_invite equals a single default token ("oracular-admin-link", or VITE_ADMIN_SIGNUP_TOKEN if set) -- a value that is not secret and, once visited, is cached permanently in localStorage (AUTH_ADMIN_INVITE_KEY). No code path in the authentication or authorization flow calls a backend endpoint to verify identity or role; this contradicts the feature suite's own normative model in features/ui_rbac_access_controls.feature, whose scenarios are written entirely in terms of backend authorize/deny decisions ("Given the backend authorizes ...", "Given the backend denies ... with reason 'rbac_denial'"). As a compounding finding, AuthUserRecord.password (line 621) is stored as plain text inside the same client-readable localStorage record (AUTH_USERS_KEY), which is further evidence that this "auth" layer is a client-only prototype, not an access-control boundary a governance reviewer could rely on.

evidence.summary: hasGovernanceAccess is a pure client-side role check with no backend call.
adminInviteGranted is granted by matching a URL query parameter against a single default token and then cached forever in localStorage.

assessments.rationale: The cited case exercises 12 classes of invalid merge/cut/load/EQ/RGS commands through the PRMC interface and asserts ValueError for each. It does not itself assert a before/after state-equality check; instead, "state unchanged" is a structural guarantee of `PRMC` being a `@dataclass(frozen=True)` whose methods return a new instance rather than mutating `self` -- since the loop never rebinds the local `cell` to a (nonexistent, because an exception was raised) return value, no mutation of the original object is possible by construction. This is a stronger guarantee than a runtime assertion, but it is a different kind of evidence than the promise's criteria literally describe, so the rationale records that distinction rather than overstating what the test itself checks.

evidence.summary: 1 passed.

views.name: current
views.description: Cross-time view accepting the repo-review agent's latest claim per promise, regardless of which revision it was observed at. `default` is pinned to a fixed historical revision for reproducibility; that pin means newly repaired or newly assessed domains (recorded at a later revision) are invisible to `default` by construction, not because anything is wrong with them. `current` is intentionally left unpinned so this session's repair and new assessments are visible. Per references/trust.md's snapshot policy, an unpinned view selects the newest per-assessor claim and must be treated as cross-time and potentially non-reproducible -- a live working picture, not a stable historical record like `default`.

views.name: default
views.description: Accept the repo-review agent's claims across the whole repository at the revision they were observed.

The initial assessor is a single automated static-analysis pass (agent/static-analysis) run against a dirty worktree during this session; the view will need a second, independent assessor (e.g. a human reviewer) before its score means anything beyond "these tools currently pass."
